word_list.word: a bad padlock invites a picklock
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ bˈæd pˈædlɑːk ɪnvˈaɪts ɐ pˈɪklɑːk

senses.definition: used to say that it is important to take proactive steps to protect oneself and one's belongings, rather than relying on weak or unreliable security measures

examples.x: The security expert warned the business owner that a bad padlock invites a picklock.

word_list.word: as you make your bed, so you must lie (up|) on it
word_list.pronunciation: æz juː mˌeɪk jʊɹ bˈɛd sˌoʊ juː mˈʌst lˈaɪ ˌʌp ˈɑːn ɪt

senses.definition: used to imply that individuals are responsible for the consequences of their actions and must accept the outcomes of their choices
used to warn that there will be consequences for one's actions, particularly if they involve indulgence or excess
used to say that offenders must be caught before they can be punished for their crimes, emphasizing the importance of taking necessary actions before consequences can occur

examples.x: The school administration understands that catching is before hanging, so they have implemented strict disciplinary policies to deter students from engaging in inappropriate behavior.

senses.definition: used to suggest that sometimes it is better to avoid prying too deeply into a situation or trying to discover too much information, as this can have negative consequences

word_list.pronunciation: ˈɛvɹi bˈʊlɪt hɐz ɐ ɪts bˈɪlɪt

senses.definition: used to highlight the idea that every action or decision has consequences or repercussions

examples.x: In business, it's important to remember that every bullet has a billet.

senses.definition: used to highlight the notion that those who engage in wrongdoing often live in fear or anticipate negative repercussions

word_list.word: give a thing, and take a thing to wear the devil's gold ring
word_list.pronunciation: ɡˈɪv ɐ θˈɪŋ ænd tˈeɪk ɐ θˈɪŋ tə wˈɛɹ ðə dˈɛvəlz ɡˈoʊld ɹˈɪŋ

senses.definition: used to imply that entering into a transaction or exchange without fully understanding the terms or evaluating the risks involved can lead to negative outcomes or undesirable consequences

word_list.word: the greater the truth, the greater the libel
word_list.pronunciation: ðə ɡɹˈeɪɾɚ ðə tɹˈuːθ ðə ɡɹˈeɪɾɚ ðə lˈaɪbəl

senses.definition: used to suggest that the more damaging or scandalous the truth is, the more likely it is to result in a lawsuit or other legal action for defamation

word_list.word: one year's seeding makes seven years' weeding
word_list.pronunciation: wˈʌn jˈɪɹz sˈiːdɪŋ mˌeɪks sˈɛvən jˈɪɹz wˈiːdɪŋ

senses.definition: used to imply that a small mistake or oversight can lead to significant problems that require a lot of effort and time to fix

word_list.word: there is no such thing as (a|) free lunch
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹ ɪz nˈoʊ sˈʌtʃ θˈɪŋ æz ɐ ɔːɹ fɹˈiː lˈʌntʃ

senses.definition: used to say that everything has a cost or consequence, even if it is not immediately apparent

word_list.word: what goes over the devil's back comes (right|) under his belly
word_list.pronunciation: wˌʌt ɡoʊz ˌoʊvɚ ðə dˈɛvəlz bˈæk kˈʌmz ɹˈaɪt ˌʌndɚ hɪz bˈɛli

senses.definition: used to emphasize that there is no escaping the repercussions of one's misdeeds
used to suggest that one's actions or behavior will have corresponding consequences or outcomes, whether they are positive or negative
used to suggest that past mistakes or wrongdoings can have long-lasting consequences and continue to haunt or affect a person even after many years have passed

examples.x: The company's previous unethical practices were exposed, and as old sins cast long shadows, it led to a loss of customers and revenue.

word_list.word: if you sell the cow, you sell her milk too
word_list.pronunciation: ɪf juː sˈɛl ðə kˈaʊ juː sˈɛl hɜː mˈɪlk tˈuː

senses.definition: used to suggest that if one gives up or sells something valuable, one also gives up the benefits and advantages that come with it
